title: Seed Starter Grow Light Stand
summary: A minimal 2x4 stand holding a grow light above a seed starter tray — two uprights, two base supports, and a top board, built from a single 2x4.
description:
This entire stand comes from one 2x4 board: two short base supports keep the whole thing from tipping, two uprights hold the top board at grow-light height above the seed tray, and the top board itself is where the light hangs or rests. There's no joinery beyond straightforward butt joints — screw the uprights to the base supports, then attach the top board across the two uprights.

**Size the upright height to your specific grow light and tray combination** — 14" is a starting point, but taller seedlings or a bulkier light fixture may need more clearance. Set the seed starter tray on the base supports, directly under the light.

This is a fast, low-cost build meant to get seedlings started indoors before the growing season — finish is optional, since this stand mostly lives somewhere out of sight during germination.

steps.title: Attach the uprights to the base
steps.body: Screw the two uprights to the two base supports, keeping them square.

steps.title: Attach the top board
steps.body: Attach the top board across the two uprights at a height that clears your grow light and seedlings.

steps.title: Set up the light and tray
steps.body: Hang or rest the grow light from the top board and place the seed starter tray on the base supports below.

unresolvedImages.alt: Simple 2x4 seed starter grow light stand
